• ⚠️ Liquidity: $275 strike, Mar 19 2027 (377 DTE), mid $61.63, delta 0.70, OI 90, spread $2.75. OI at 90 is thin — this is borderline illiquid. The $2.75 spread on a ~$62 option is ~4.5%, which is tolerable but not great.

RSI(14) Daily: 40.8 | Weekly: 40.9

1) Strategy Selector

IV environment: ATM IV at 31.5% vs HV1y 30.3% — IV is roughly in line, not particularly rich or cheap. With RSI in the low 40s on both timeframes, GOOG is leaning oversold without being extreme. At moderate conviction, the flowchart says:

• Sell CSP? IV is near fair value, not rich — premium won't be spectacular, but the stock is pulling back toward strong support at ~$297. Acceptable setup if you want to own shares.
• Buy LEAP? RSI is approaching oversold territory but not there yet (sub-35 would be ideal). IV isn't cheap enough to make LEAPs a screaming deal. Wait for a deeper pullback.
• Both? Not yet — conditions favor one or the other, not a dual commit.
• Wait? This is borderline. If $297 support breaks, better entries exist below.

Verdict: Lean toward CSP if $297 support holds; otherwise wait for a cleaner setup.

---

2) Cash-Secured Put Strike Finder (Exp: Apr 2, 2026 — 28 DTE)

Key support: $296.70 (20d low = 12w low — strong confluence)
Chosen strike: $280 (δ = −0.20, IV = 35.5%)
Premium (mid): $3.78
Effective cost basis if assigned: $280 − $3.78 = $276.22
Capital required: $28,000

Would I want 100 shares at $276? That's 8.2% below current price and well below the $297 support cluster — a level GOOG hasn't seen since mid-2025. For a 2-3 year hold, $276 is attractive.

Honest assessment: The $3.78 premium on $28K capital = 1.35% return in 28 days (17.6% annualized). That's decent but not exciting for the risk. The bid/ask is tight ($3.70/$3.85) and OI is 384 — adequate liquidity. This trade works if you're genuinely willing to own GOOG at $276. If you're just collecting premium, the reward-to-risk is marginal given moderate conviction.

---

3) Covered Call Timing Setup (Cost basis: $300.91)

Nearest resistance: $332.64 (20d high), then $350.15 (60d/12w high)

Premium (mid): $2.52

Honest assessment: Wait. RSI is in the low 40s — GOOG is pulling back and likely to bounce before it makes sense to cap upside. Selling a call now means collecting $2.52 while the stock could easily snap back to $325-330 on any positive catalyst. The textbook move is to sell covered calls when RSI is overbought (>65) and price is stalling at resistance. Neither condition is met. Skip this trade for now.

Verdict: NO — conditions not aligned. Wait for RSI to drop below 35 (daily) and/or IV to compress below HV1y. A pullback to $290 or below with IV compression would be the ideal LEAP entry. You're close but not there yet.

• Both? No — don't sell cheap premium.
• Wait? Could wait for RSI < 35, but conditions are already better than GOOG and NVDA.

Verdict: LEAP is the standout play. IV is cheap, price is near support, RSI is leaning oversold. At moderate conviction, a half-sized LEAP position with a plan to add on further weakness makes sense.

You're being paid for 41% vol to absorb 60% vol risk. That's a losing proposition over time. OI is 998 and spread is tight ($6.85/$6.95), so liquidity is fine. However, the $370 strike has only 27 OI, which means potential slippage. Consider the $350 or $400 strikes for better liquidity. At moderate conviction, enter a half-size position and plan to add if TSLA pulls back to $385 support with RSI below 35.
